raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The legislation AB 791 would provide $200 million in new tax credits for the construction of affordable housing in Opportunity Zones.",
    "AB 791 would provide $100 million in new tax credits to preserve affordable housing stock in Opportunity Zones.",
    "Opportunity Zones were created as part of the 2017 federal tax overhaul, offering significant tax benefits to investors in low income census tracts.",
    "California has designated 879 Opportunity Zones across the state that are expected to benefit from billions of dollars in private investment.",
    "There are concerns that the federal legislation on Opportunity Zones may not guarantee public benefits from the investments.",
    "Critics argue that Opportunity Zones may discourage affordable housing production and lead to the displacement of long-time low income residents.",
    "AB 791 seeks to provide a national model for how states can incentivize investments that yield public benefits.",
    "The Opportunity Zone proposal in AB 791 can help build more affordable housing and protect low-income families.",
    "Opportunity Zones allow for new types of investors, including High Net Worth Individuals and Corporations, in the affordable housing sector.",
    "The Low Income Housing Tax Credit program has mainly attracted banks as the primary purchasers of credits."
  ]
}
```
